calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Judgment Under Ccp Sec 663(A)
Off Calendar Apr-08-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Petitioner MARIDOL MENDONES, in pro per present.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: PETITIONER MARIDOL MENDONES' Motion To Vacate Judgment Under Ccp Sec 663(A).

Matter discussed. The Court notes Petitioner failed to contest the tentative ruling. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion to vacate judgment under CCP 663 is denied. Judgment was entered in this case after a demurrer was sustained without leave to amend. But a "section 663 motion simply does not lie to vacate a judgment following a demurrer sustained without leave to amend." (Payne v. Rader (2008) 167 Cal.App.4th 1569, 1575.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER
Off Calendar Jan-16-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. SHARON M. NAGLE, Esq. of Bold, Polisner, Maddow, Nelson & Judson (Tel: 925) 933-7777) for defemdamts;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS HON. TANI CANTIL-SAKAUYE, JORGE NAVARRETE, AND FLORENTINO JIMENEZ'S DEMURRER.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' (HON. TANI CANTIL-SAKAUYE, JORGE NAVARRETE, FLORENTINO JIMENEZ) demurrer to the amended petition is sustained without leave to amend. (CCP 430.10(e).)

The gravamen of this action arises from the California Supreme Court's March 23, 2022, order denying petitioner's fee waiver application in a prior case. Defendant Chief Justice Cantil-Sakauye is absolutely immune from a suit for damages allegedly arising out of official court rulings as a matter of law. "The decisions of this state uniformly and consistently grant immunity from civil suit to judges in the exercise of their judicial functions. [citation] That is true even if the acts are in excess of the jurisdiction of the judge and are alleged to have been done maliciously and corruptly." (Tagliavia v. County of Los Angeles (1980) 112 Cal.App.3d 759, 761.)

Additionally, defendants Navarrete and Jimenez are entitled to quasi-judicial immunity for their alleged conduct regarding the fee waiver order. The doctrine of judicial immunity has been extended to individuals (such as defendants) who "served functions integral to the judicial process." (Howard v. Drapkin (1990) 222 Cal.App.3d 843, 856.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT (PETITION)
Off Calendar Nov-29-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: JUDICIAL DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Off calendar. The declaration of Sharon Nagel shows that the parties have not met and conferred as required by Code of Civil Procedure 430.41. Plaintiff is ordered to comply with the law by meeting and conferring immediately; it is not lawful to ignore contacts from defense counsel. The new date for responding to the first amended complaint is December 27, 2023. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (302/RBU)
